sourceMovie.title: Get Shorty
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Get Shorty for the same reason it broke through in 1995: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Barry Sonnenfeld keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Bad Boys
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Get Shorty clicked for you, Bad Boys (1995) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and thriller, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: American Psycho
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Get Shorty clicked for you, American Psycho (2000) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Get Shorty?
faq.answer: Try Bad Boys, In Order of Disappearance, American Psycho—each shares DNA with Get Shorty while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Get Shorty?
faq.answer: Get Shorty (1995) is best described as Comedy, Thriller, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Get Shorty part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Chili Palmer Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
